_meta.title: Time Complexity Classes — P, NP, EXP

questions.question: A language L is in NP if and only if:
options: Some TM decides L in exponential time
Some polynomial-time verifier V accepts (x, c) for some certificate c iff x ∈ L
Every TM that recognizes L halts in polynomial time
L can be decided by a nondeterministic TM in exponential time
questions.explanation: NP is the class of languages with a polynomial-time verifier: x ∈ L iff there exists a polynomial-length certificate c such that V(x, c) accepts.

questions.question: Which relationship is known to be true?
options: P = NP
NP = EXP
P ⊆ NP ⊆ EXP
EXP ⊆ NP
questions.explanation: P ⊆ NP (solving is a special case of verifying), and NP ⊆ EXP (brute-force search over poly-length certificates is exponential). Whether these inclusions are strict is open.

questions.question: For the SAT problem, which statement is correct?
options: Verification of a satisfying assignment is exponential, but solving is polynomial
Verification is polynomial (evaluate the formula under the assignment), but solving is believed exponential
Both verification and solving are polynomial
Both verification and solving are undecidable
questions.explanation: Given a truth assignment, evaluating a CNF formula is O(n). Finding a satisfying assignment requires brute-force search over 2^n assignments in the worst case.

questions.question: If P = NP, which consequence follows?
options: The halting problem becomes decidable
Every NP-complete problem has a polynomial-time algorithm
EXP collapses to P
Rice's theorem is falsified
questions.explanation: If P = NP, then every problem in NP (including all NP-complete problems) can be decided in polynomial time. This would break most cryptography but does not affect undecidability results.

questions.question: What is the class P in complexity theory?
options: Problems solvable by a Turing machine using polynomial space
Problems solvable by a deterministic Turing machine in polynomial time
Problems that can be verified in polynomial time
Problems solvable by a nondeterministic Turing machine in polynomial time
questions.explanation: P is the class of decision problems solvable by a deterministic Turing machine in polynomial time. NP (nondeterministic polynomial) includes problems whose solutions can be verified in polynomial time. Whether P = NP is the most important open question in computer science.
